message:
fix: loading flicker, DynamicCard state desync, and missing delete confirm (#5232)

* fix: prevent multi-render loading flicker race condition (#5206)

Add MIN_SKELETON_DISPLAY_MS (200ms) constant that ensures the skeleton
is shown for a minimum duration on initial mount. This prevents the
flicker where childDataState starts null (showing skeleton), then child
reports state via useLayoutEffect causing a re-render that briefly shows
content before the skeleton timeout completes.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: report DynamicCard internal loading state to CardWrapper (#5208)

Tier1CardRuntime and Tier2CardRuntime now report their internal loading
states (apiLoading, compiling) via useReportCardDataState so CardWrapper
header accurately reflects the card body state instead of showing
"Loaded" while the body is still "Compiling..." or "Fetching...".

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: add confirmation dialog before deleting cluster groups (#5197)

The cards/ClusterGroups component was deleting groups immediately on
click without asking for confirmation. Add a ConfirmDialog (danger
variant) that requires explicit user confirmation before deletion,
matching the pattern already used in clusters/components/ClusterGroups.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

         ? (cardDefinition?.staticData || [])
         : apiData)
 
+  // Report loading state to CardWrapper so header stays in sync with body (#5208)
+  const isApiSource = !isInvalidConfig && cardDefinition?.dataSource === 'api'
+  useReportCardDataState({
+    isFailed: !!apiError,
+    consecutiveFailures: apiError ? 1 : 0,
+    errorMessage: apiError ?? undefined,
+    isLoading: isApiSource ? apiLoading : false,
+    hasData: isApiSource ? apiData.length > 0 : true,
+    isDemoData: false,
+  })
+
   // Fetch API data if needed
   useEffect(() => {
     if (isInvalidConfig || isMissingEndpoint) return
@@ -341,6 +352,16 @@ export function Tier2CardRuntime({ definition, config }: Tier2Props) {
   const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null)
   const cleanupRef = useRef<(() => void) | undefined>(undefined)
 
+  // Report internal loading/error state to CardWrapper so header stays in sync (#5208)
+  useReportCardDataState({
+    isFailed: !!error,
+    consecutiveFailures: error ? 1 : 0,
+    errorMessage: error ?? undefined,
+    isLoading: compiling,
+    hasData: !!CardComponent,
+    isDemoData: false,
+  })
+
   useEffect(() => {
     let cancelled = false

@@ -181,6 +181,11 @@ export const SKELETON_DELAY_MS = 100
 /** Timeout for initial render measurement (150ms) */
 export const INITIAL_RENDER_TIMEOUT_MS = 150
 
+/** Minimum duration to show skeleton on initial mount before transitioning to content (200ms).
+ * Prevents flicker when child reports state via useLayoutEffect causing a re-render
+ * that briefly shows content before the skeleton timeout completes (#5206). */
+export const MIN_SKELETON_DISPLAY_MS = 200
+

  * hook cancellation, or error during data fetching), this timeout forces it to exit
